name: Eddy Arnold
abstract: Richard Edward Arnold (May 15, 1918 – May 8, 2008) was an American country music singer. He was a Nashville sound (country/popular music) innovator of the late 1950s, and scored 147 songs on the Billboard country music charts, second only to George Jones.

about_quotes.speaker: Alberta Hunter
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Arnold, I told you, did I Want to Thank You, Lord, with a choir... He did it for Victor... So I've had some good songs, you know. I mean good people do my material.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Spottswood
about_quotes.evidence: Doc Watson named his son Eddie Merle because he was naming him after Eddie Arnold and Merle Travis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Spottswood interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 13, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Mancuso
about_quotes.evidence: You know, when he worked with Eddie Arnold, he only worked with Eddie Arnold.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Mancuso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 10, 2013
